content.claim: Segregation and selection in Hungary are amplified by privatization and church-school growth rather than by parental school choice per se; the share of church-run primary schools rose from 8.6% to 16.6% and secondary schools from 10% to 26.1% between 2010 and 2021.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Cross-country comparison shows no visible relationship between free choice and selection strength, suggesting Hungary-specific drivers, but does not identify which policies caused the change
Church-school percentage covers all denominational schools, not the 6-/8-year track specifically
Causal claim about privatization/church-school effect on selection intensity is based on review summary rather than direct quantitative model
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
